title: Bakki Sourthol
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Sourthol.png]] |name=Bakki Sourthol |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars Campaign Pack]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=Fair |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Reekeene's Roughnecks]] **[[Task Force on Alliance Security]]''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|If TIE forces were allowed to operate on their own terms, the Alliance starfighter forces would surely vanish from space.
We do not allow the Empire to operate on its own terms, and so we keep an edge. Read this report and hone that edge.|Introduction to Bakki Sourthol's report on TIE Fighters|{{JournalCite|10|Alliance Intelligence Report: TIE Fighters}}}} [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] '''Bakki Sourthol''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and [[Officer/Legends|officer]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], flying a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] through the [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]] as the [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]] of [[Reekeene's Roughnecks]]. Sourthol sometimes worked along with special operations and intelligence, writing reports on the threats posed by the [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]] [[Yansan]] and by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. Affable and animated, Sourthol was also good at playing [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|One of the reasons Intell has asked me to contribute to Yansan's dossier is because of my encounter with him in the Fakir sector: he actually attempted to disable some of our transports while we were still inside!|Lieutenant Commander Bakki Sourthol, Alliance to Restore the Republic|}} Bakki Sourthol, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] because his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] political beliefs prevented him from entering the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]]. An experienced [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Sourthol commanded an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] Squadron in the [[Bakchou arm]] of [[Fakir sector/Legends|Fakir sector]] as a member of [[Reekeene's Roughnecks]]. His callsign was [[Red Leader/Legends|Red Leader]]. He also became a [[Lieutenant Commander/Legends|Lieutenant Commander]] working for Special Operations. During his service in the Alliance, Sourthol identified [[Yansan]], a [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteur]], trying to disable Rebel [[Transport/Legends|transports]] with several Rebels on board, including Sourthol himself. Yansan was stopped before he could finish the task, but he managed to escape. During this event, Sourthol noticed that Yansan had a limp, preferring the left leg. On [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], due to some events in the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]], Sourthol obtained important information about other threats to the Rebel Alliance. Due to this, he was temporarily relieved from his duties on Fakir so he could report to the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]]. Sourthol wrote about Yansan and other threats. During this time, he also worked along with Lieutenants [[Anson Blazer]], [[Deeve]] and [[Tura Raftican]], and Special Agent [[Tay Vanis]]. Later, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Bakki Sourthol of the [[Red Squadron]] wrote a brief report on the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] of starfighters used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Although it was an Intelligence Report, it was not restricted to Intel agents. Sourthol recognized that his report was uncomprehensive, and suggested another, more extensive report by [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] to complete the research. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Clear skies!|Introduction to Bakki Sourthol's report on TIE Fighters|}} Sourthol was a friendly, cheerful and fearless man and a mean [[Sabacc/Legends|Sabacc]] player. He was also an excellent pilot, an observant Intelligence officer and an expert in military technology. He was familiar with the tactics and history of TIE technology. While writing a report on them, he used ancient military proverbs as epigraph to some sections. ==Behind the scenes== Sourthol first appeared in ''[[Star Wars Campaign Pack]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', first published in the [[1988]] and then reprinted along with other material in ''[[Classic Campaigns]]'' in [[1994]]. He was then mentioned in further role-playing supplements, including ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' ([[1995]]). In ''Star Wars Campaign Pack'', his hair is described as "fair" but a graphic depiction of Sourthol by artist [[Mike Vilardi]] in ''Alliance Intelligence Reports'' shows him with apparent dark hair. ''[[Alliance Intelligence Report: TIE Fighters]]'' (published in [[1996]] in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' #10]]) presented as a report written by Sourthol, apparently is a document written in times of the Rebel Alliance. However, Sourthol says that Wedge Antilles' report is available through the [[Ministry of Education]], suggesting that at this point Sourthol's party held control over official institutions, and thus that the report was written in times of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Campaign Pack]]'' *''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' *''[[Classic Campaigns]]'' *{{JournalCite|10|Alliance Intelligence Report: TIE Fighters}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sourthol, Bakki}} [[Category:Alliance Fleet officers]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Reekeene's Roughnecks personnel]] [[Category:Task Force on Alliance Security personnel]]
